calendar.matters:
MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Sep-10-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT LAIA TUNU's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Specially appearing defendant Laia Tunu's motion to quash service of summons is granted.

"When a defendant challenges the court's personal jurisdiction on the ground of improper service of process, the burden is on the plaintiff to prove the existence of jurisdiction by proving, inter alia, the facts requisite to an effective service." (Summers v. McClanahan (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 403, 413 [internal quotation marks, citation, and footnote omitted].) Tunu presents evidence that the summons was not left in the presence of a member of her household at least 18 years of age. (Tunu Dec. on Motion to Set Aside, para. 2.) Plaintiff Meriwest Credit Union does not present evidence that service was effected in compliance with the Code of Civil Procedure, sec. 415.20, subdivision (b), and does not oppose the motion.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Jul-30-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT LAIA TUNU's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Specially appearing defendant Laia Tunu's motion to set aside default and default judgment is granted. Tunu presents evidence that she was never served with the summons and complaint in this action. Plaintiff Meriwest Credit Union presents no evidence to contest Tunu's evidence. A judgment based on improper service is void and may be set aside at any time. (California Capital Ins. Co. v. Hoehn (2024) 17 Cal.5th 207, 226.) Although it is not required here, Tunu also presents evidence concerning why she did not immediately seek to set aside the default after receiving notice of wage garnishment, and she has demonstrated diligence under the circumstances. The default and default judgment are set aside.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Jun-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MERIWEST CREDIT UNION's HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION.

The judgment debtor's Claim of Exemption is granted in its entirety. (CCP 706.051(b).)

Court to prepare order.

Visiting Judge: John True; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/JT)
